steps.title: Isolate the 14x term
steps.content: Start with $$14x+60=200.$$ Subtract $60$ from both sides: $$14x=140.$$

steps.title: Match an equivalent equation
steps.content: Any equation with the same solution can be written as $14x=140$, which gives $x=10$.

steps.title: Why this is equivalent
steps.content: Subtracting the same number from both sides keeps the solution unchanged. The equation $14x=140$ is therefore the same problem with the constant term removed from the left side.
